neverRemove.name: Fabric API
neverRemove.notes: The Fabric mod loader's standard library. Removing it fails every Fabric mod.

neverRemove.name: Fabric Language Kotlin
neverRemove.notes: Kotlin runtime for Fabric mods. Declared optional/optional upstream, but any Kotlin mod left behind will fail to load without it.

neverRemove.name: Architectury API
neverRemove.notes: Cross-loader abstraction layer; declared required on both sides. REI and many others hard-depend on it.

neverRemove.name: Cloth Config API
neverRemove.notes: Config library. Filenames appear as both cloth-config-*.jar and ClothConfig*.jar, hence two prefixes.

neverRemove.name: Kotlin for Forge
neverRemove.notes: Kotlin runtime for Forge/NeoForge. Filenames are kotlinforforge-6.3.0-all.jar.

neverRemove.name: Forge Config API Port
neverRemove.notes: Ports Forge's config system to other loaders. Filenames are ForgeConfigAPIPort-v26.2.1-mc26.2.x-Fabric.jar; matching is case-insensitive.

neverRemove.name: Balm
neverRemove.notes: Blay's cross-loader abstraction layer (Waystones, Cooking for Blockheads, ...).

neverRemove.name: Bookshelf
neverRemove.notes: Darkhax's library, declared required on both sides. Note the Modrinth slug is 'bookshelf-lib' — the bare 'bookshelf' slug is an unrelated Paper plugin.

neverRemove.name: Collective
neverRemove.notes: Serilum's shared library; every Serilum mod hard-depends on it.

neverRemove.name: Resourceful Lib
neverRemove.notes: Team Resourceful's library, declared required on both sides.

neverRemove.name: Framework
neverRemove.notes: MrCrayfish's library mod. No Modrinth project exists, so this is slug- and filename-matched only. The bare 'framework-' prefix is broad, which is acceptable here: over-matching in neverRemove only protects extra files.

neverRemove.name: MixinExtras
neverRemove.notes: Usually jar-in-jar'd, but sometimes shipped loose. No Modrinth project, so this is filename-matched only. Removing it breaks every mod using its mixin syntax.

neverRemove.name: Sinytra Connector
neverRemove.notes: Runs Fabric mods on NeoForge. Filenames appear as both connector-*.jar and Connector-*.jar; matching is case-insensitive.

neverRemove.name: Forgified Fabric API
neverRemove.notes: The Fabric API implementation Connector depends on. Declared required on both sides.

rules.name: Sodium
rules.notes: Rendering engine replacement. No server-side code path at all.

rules.name: Iris Shaders
rules.notes: Shader loader. Shaderpacks live in shaderpacks/, which the cleaner does not scan.

rules.name: Indium
rules.notes: Sodium addon providing the Fabric Rendering API. Useless without Sodium.

rules.name: Embeddium

evidence.detail: Forge/NeoForge-side port of Sodium; Modrinth also tags fabric, so the loader list is kept wide rather than assuming forge-only.

rules.notes: Sodium port for Forge/NeoForge.

rules.name: Oculus
rules.notes: Iris port for Forge. Pairs with Rubidium/Embeddium.

rules.name: Rubidium
rules.notes: Predecessor of Embeddium; Sodium port for Forge.

rules.name: Canvas Renderer
rules.notes: Alternative rendering engine.

rules.name: Enhanced Block Entities

evidence.detail: Separate NeoForge/Forge ports exist as distinct Modrinth projects (HlLmiJmt, wxtGAtU7); the filename prefix covers all three.

rules.notes: Block entity rendering optimisation.

rules.name: Entity Culling

evidence.detail: fabric.mod.json environment="* (absent, defaults to both)" (mod id entityculling)
fabric.mod.json omits `environment`, but the author declares server_side=unsupported and the mod only skips client-side rendering.

rules.notes: Skips rendering of entities hidden behind blocks. Purely a render-path change.

rules.name: ImmediatelyFast
rules.notes: Two independent signals agree: declared client environment and unsupported server side.

rules.name: Sodium Extra
rules.notes: Sodium options addon.

rules.name: Reese's Sodium Options
rules.notes: Replacement video settings screen for Sodium.

rules.name: Continuity
rules.notes: Connected textures. Resource-pack driven, no server component.

rules.name: More Culling
rules.notes: Additional face-culling in the render path.

rules.name: Cull Less Leaves
rules.notes: Leaf-block render culling.

rules.name: Dynamic FPS
rules.notes: Reduces framerate when the game window loses focus. Meaningless headless.

rules.name: Sodium Shadowy Path Blocks
rules.notes: Restores vanilla path-block shading under Sodium.

rules.name: Borderless Mining
rules.notes: Changes the OS window mode. Nothing to do on a server.

rules.name: LambDynamicLights

evidence.detail: Purely a client-side lightmap effect; it does not write light data to the world.

rules.notes: Dynamic lighting is a client render effect and is not persisted.

rules.name: Animatica
rules.notes: Implements OptiFine animated textures. Resource-pack feature.

rules.name: Colormatic
rules.notes: Custom colours from resource packs.

rules.name: CIT Resewn
rules.notes: Implements OptiFine CIT (custom item textures).

rules.name: Entity Texture Features (ETF)
rules.notes: OptiFine random/emissive entity textures.

rules.name: Entity Model Features (EMF)
rules.notes: OptiFine custom entity models.

rules.name: Custom Entity Models (CEM)
rules.notes: OptiFine CEM implementation.

rules.name: Visuality
rules.notes: Extra particle effects, spawned client-side only.

rules.name: Falling Leaves
rules.notes: Decorative leaf particles.

rules.name: Particle Rain
rules.notes: Replaces weather rendering with particles.

rules.name: Better Clouds
rules.notes: Cloud rendering replacement.

rules.name: 3D Skin Layers
rules.notes: Renders the player skin's second layer in 3D.

rules.name: Capes
rules.notes: Fetches capes from third-party services for local rendering.

rules.name: Chat Heads

evidence.detail: An optional server-side companion exists for name resolution on some platforms, but the mod jar itself is client-render only.

rules.notes: Draws player heads next to chat messages.

rules.name: Not Enough Animations
rules.notes: Third-person animation polish, rendered locally.

rules.name: First-person Model
rules.notes: Renders your own body in first person.

rules.name: Shoulder Surfing Reloaded
rules.notes: Third-person camera replacement.

rules.name: Boat Item View
rules.notes: Hides the held item while in a boat.

rules.name: Mouse Tweaks
rules.notes: Sends ordinary container clicks; the server sees nothing unusual.

rules.name: Inventory Profiles Next

evidence.detail: Depends on libIPN, which is also client-only and is listed separately.

rules.notes: Automates inventory sorting using vanilla click packets.

rules.name: Controlling
rules.notes: Adds search to the keybind screen.

rules.name: Mod Menu

evidence.detail: Frequently declared as a dependency by client mods. Because it is stripped together with its dependents this is safe, but it is a common source of 'missing dependency' reports when a pack is stripped inconsistently.

rules.notes: Adds the mods screen to the title menu.

rules.name: BetterF3

evidence.detail: Distinct from the Forge-only project 'Better F3' (V8TerSIX); both are client-only, but only this id is matched here.

rules.notes: Reskins the F3 debug overlay.

rules.name: Zoomify
rules.notes: Camera zoom keybind.

rules.name: Ok Zoomer
rules.notes: Camera zoom keybind.

rules.name: Logical Zoom
rules.notes: Camera zoom keybind.

rules.name: WI Zoom
rules.notes: Camera zoom keybind.

rules.name: Amecs
rules.notes: Keybind modifier support.

rules.name: Raised
rules.notes: Shifts the hotbar rendering position.

rules.name: Detail Armor Bar
rules.notes: Redraws the armour HUD from data the client already has.

rules.name: Legendary Tooltips

evidence.detail: Depends on Iceberg, which declares server_side=required and is listed separately as client-optional. Strip this without stripping Iceberg.

rules.notes: Tooltip restyling.

rules.name: Better Advancements
rules.notes: Replacement advancement screen. Advancement data still comes from the server.

rules.name: Better Mount HUD
rules.notes: Mount health/jump HUD rendering.

rules.name: Light Overlay
rules.notes: Draws spawn-safety markers from client-side light data.

rules.name: Freecam
rules.notes: Detached camera. Client-side only, and usually unwanted on a server anyway.

rules.name: CraftPresence
rules.notes: Discord Rich Presence integration.

rules.name: Screenshot to Clipboard
rules.notes: Copies screenshots to the OS clipboard.

rules.name: Auth Me

evidence.detail: Unrelated to server-side login plugins such as AuthMe Reloaded, which are server-required. Matched by project id to avoid that collision.

rules.notes: Re-authenticates the client's Mojang session in-game.

rules.name: AmbientSounds

evidence.detail: Depends on CreativeCore, which is dual-sided and must not be stripped with it.

rules.notes: Ambient sound loops generated locally.

rules.name: Presence Footsteps
rules.notes: Replaces footstep sounds client-side.

rules.name: Sound Physics Remastered

evidence.detail: Author declares server_side=optional and the jar loads on both sides, so this is not marked verified. All reverb/occlusion processing happens in the client audio pipeline; removing the jar from a server does not change gameplay.

rules.notes: Downgraded to 'high' because the author does not declare the server side unsupported. Still auto-selectable, but the residual risk is recorded here.

rules.name: Xaero's Minimap
rules.reason: Client-rendered minimap; server side is optional extras only

evidence.detail: server_side=optional covers fair-play/waypoint-sharing integrations. The jar is inert on a dedicated server that does not use them, so this stays client-only, but at 'high' rather than 'verified'.

rules.notes: The Xaero server-side jar enforces fair-play restrictions (cave mode, entity radar, waypoint sharing) — the same admin control surface that keeps JourneyMap at client-optional. Stripping it silently disables an anti-cheat control an operator installed deliberately.

rules.name: Xaero's World Map
rules.reason: Client-rendered world map; server side is optional extras only
rules.notes: Same as Xaero's Minimap: the companion server jar is a deliberate operator control, not dead client weight.

rules.name: VoxelMap (Updated)

evidence.detail: Modrinth also tags paper/spigot/purpur loaders on this project, which contradicts server_side=unsupported. Kept at 'high' because of that inconsistency rather than 'verified'.

rules.notes: Modrinth tags paper/spigot/purpur loaders on this project, which contradicts server_side=unsupported — a project publishing Paper artifacts has a server-side half. When sideness is not clear the answer is client-optional, not a guess in the strip direction.

rules.name: JourneyMap

evidence.detail: a 'journeymap-paper-26.2-6.0.1.jar' build is published, and forge/neoforge server builds provide the JourneyMap Server admin features
JourneyMap Server controls what clients may map (cave mapping, radar, death waypoints). Stripping it silently removes an admin control surface.

rules.notes: NEVER auto-strip. Surface it so an operator can decide.

rules.name: Just Enough Items (JEI)
rules.reason: Has server-side recipe transfer and cheat-mode handling

evidence.detail: JEI registers server-bound packets for recipe transfer ('+' button) and cheat mode. Server-side JEI plugins from other mods also register against it. Removing it can break recipe transfer and can fail mod dependency checks.

rules.notes: Very commonly mistaken for client-only. It is not. NEVER auto-strip.

rules.name: Roughly Enough Items (REI)
rules.reason: Has server-side recipe transfer and cheat-mode handling

evidence.detail: A dedicated 'Roughly Enough Items Server Component' (OM4ZYSws) exists with client_side=unsupported/server_side=required, which confirms REI has a real server half. REI also requires Architectury API on both sides.

rules.notes: NEVER auto-strip. Note REI's own filename is 'RoughlyEnoughItems-'.

rules.name: EMI

evidence.detail: EMI uses a server-side component for recipe tree/crafting integration and for sync in cheat mode.

rules.notes: NEVER auto-strip.

rules.name: Jade
rules.reason: Server sends the block/entity data Jade displays

evidence.detail: fabric.mod.json environment="* (environment key absent)" (mod id jade)
Jade requests detailed block-entity and mob data from the server over its own channel. Without the server half, tooltips silently degrade to vanilla data.

rules.notes: NEVER auto-strip. Also the target of many addon mods that expect it server-side.

rules.name: WTHIT
rules.reason: Server sends the block/entity data WTHIT displays

evidence.detail: Same architecture as Jade/WAILA: a server component supplies the tooltip data.

rules.notes: NEVER auto-strip.

rules.name: AppleSkin
rules.reason: Server syncs saturation and exhaustion values

evidence.detail: Saturation and exhaustion are not sent to vanilla clients. AppleSkin's server half exists specifically to sync them. Strip it and the HUD shows wrong values.

rules.notes: Classic false positive: it looks like a HUD mod, but the data comes from the server.

rules.name: Enchantment Descriptions

evidence.detail: Depends on Bookshelf, which is dual-sided and protected by neverRemove.

rules.notes: Tooltip mod, but the author declares server support; not auto-selected.

rules.name: No Chat Reports
rules.reason: Server side controls chat signing policy

evidence.detail: On the server it disables chat signature enforcement and advertises safety status to clients. Removing it re-enables reportable chat, which is a policy change an operator may not expect.

rules.notes: NEVER auto-strip. This one has a real, deliberate server-side effect.

rules.name: Lithium

evidence.detail: Lithium optimises entity AI, chunk ticking, collision and redstone — almost all of it server-side. Stripping it directly reduces server TPS.

rules.notes: Do not strip. Grouped with Sodium in most packs, which is exactly why naive 'performance mod' heuristics get this wrong.

rules.name: Starlight

evidence.detail: Starlight rewrites the light engine on both sides. Removing it from the server loses the entire benefit and can change lighting behaviour mid-world.

rules.notes: Do not strip.

rules.name: Krypton

evidence.detail: Optimises the network stack, which is predominantly a server concern.

rules.notes: Do not strip.

rules.name: FerriteCore

evidence.detail: Deduplicates blockstate and model memory. On a large modpack server this is one of the highest-value mods to keep.

rules.notes: Do not strip. Frequently mislabelled as a client FPS mod.

rules.name: Memory Leak Fix
rules.notes: Do not strip.

rules.name: LazyDFU

evidence.detail: Defers DataFixerUpper initialisation, which shortens startup on servers too.

rules.notes: Do not strip. Deprecated on modern versions but still present in older packs.

rules.name: Fastload

evidence.detail: Alters world/chunk load behaviour at startup on both sides.

rules.notes: Do not strip.

rules.name: Debugify

evidence.detail: Fixes a configurable set of MC-xxxxx bugs, many of which are server-side behaviour fixes. Removing it from only one side can desync behaviour.

rules.notes: Do not strip.

rules.name: Prism

evidence.detail: Colour-handling library consumed by client mods (Iceberg, Legendary Tooltips). Safe to strip only when its dependents are also stripped — which is why this is 'high' rather than 'verified'.

rules.notes: Library. Dual-sided content mods (Resourceful Lib family) declare it as a required dependency, and loader dependency resolution hard-fails on an unsatisfied depends entry regardless of side. server_side=unsupported is the library author describing their own mod, not evidence that removing it is safe for whatever is left behind. Surfaced, never auto-stripped, because nothing in this pipeline reads dependency graphs.

rules.name: Searchables

evidence.detail: Search-widget library used by client-facing GUIs.

rules.notes: Library. Same dependent caveat as Prism: dual-sided Create-family content mods declare it required. Surfaced, never auto-stripped, until a consumer actually parses depends/mods.toml from the surviving jars.

rules.name: YetAnotherConfigLib (YACL)

evidence.detail: Primarily a client config-screen library, but declared optional on the server and depended on by dual-sided mods. Not worth the dependency risk to strip.

rules.notes: Library. Small; leave it alone.

rules.name: Konkrete
rules.notes: Library used by FancyMenu and friends.

rules.name: Iceberg
rules.notes: Library. Declared required on both sides — do not strip.

rules.name: Placebo

evidence.detail: Apotheosis and other content mods hard-depend on it server-side.

rules.notes: Library. Do not strip.

rules.name: Patchouli

evidence.detail: Looks like a book-rendering client mod, but content mods declare it as a hard dependency and the server validates it at load.

rules.notes: Do not strip. Removing it fails dependency resolution for its dependents.

rules.name: Quark

evidence.detail: Adds blocks and items. Stripping it desyncs the registry and corrupts placed content — the exact failure mode this list exists to prevent.

rules.notes: Do not strip under any circumstances.

rules.name: Distant Horizons
rules.reason: Has a real server-side LOD component

evidence.detail: Since 2.x, Distant Horizons can run server-side to generate and serve LODs to clients. Stripping it removes that capability silently.

rules.notes: NEVER auto-strip. Was safely client-only in 1.x, which is why old lists get it wrong.

rules.name: ReplayMod

evidence.detail: Records the client's inbound packet stream to a local file.

rules.notes: Recording and rendering are entirely local.

rules.name: Litematica

evidence.detail: Depends on MaLiLib, which is also client-only and listed separately. Some servers run a companion plugin (litematica-printer / easyplace helpers); those are separate artefacts.

rules.notes: Schematic overlay rendered locally.

rules.name: MiniHUD
rules.notes: Information overlay drawn from client state.

rules.name: Tweakeroo
rules.notes: Client-side tweaks; all actions are expressed as vanilla packets.

rules.name: MaLiLib

evidence.detail: Support library for the masa mod family (Litematica, MiniHUD, Tweakeroo), all of which are themselves client-only.

rules.notes: Library. Safe to remove ONLY if every masa-family dependent is also removed; itemscroller and others are not in this list. Leave one behind and Fabric refuses to launch. Surfaced, never auto-stripped.

rules.name: libIPN
rules.notes: Library for Inventory Profiles Next. Safe only alongside IPN itself. Nothing here evaluates that precondition, so it is surfaced rather than auto-stripped.

rules.name: CreativeCore

evidence.detail: Dependency of AmbientSounds and of dual-sided mods such as Little Tiles. Stripping it with AmbientSounds would break those.

rules.notes: Library. Do not strip even when AmbientSounds is stripped.

rules.name: Controllable

evidence.detail: Not published on Modrinth, so there is no server_side declaration to cite. The mod binds gamepad input to the client input handler.
No CurseForge numeric project id is recorded: inventing one would create a high-precedence matcher that could hit an unrelated project.

rules.notes: CurseForge-only. Slug and filename matching only, by design.

rules.name: Smooth Scrolling Everywhere

evidence.detail: CurseForge-primary. Purely a GUI rendering tweak; no registry or network use.

rules.notes: CurseForge-only. Slug and filename matching only.

rules.name: Inventory HUD+
rules.reason: HUD overlay rendered from the client's own inventory state

evidence.detail: Project id intentionally omitted: the Modrinth mirror and the CurseForge original are different publications of the same mod and the ids are not interchangeable. Slug matching is the honest option here.

rules.notes: Filename prefix 'inventoryhud.' is intentional — releases are named InventoryHUD.forge.1.20.1.jar with dots, not hyphens.

rules.name: OptiFine
rules.reason: Client-only renderer; never valid in a server mods folder

evidence.detail: OptiFine is distributed only from optifine.net and has no server build. A dedicated server that loads it crashes or ignores it.
Matched by filename only because OptiFine has no Modrinth or CurseForge project. The regex is anchored and requires a separator to avoid matching unrelated names.

rules.notes: Filename-only rule. Matching is case-insensitive, so OptiFine_1.20.1_HD_U_I6.jar is covered.

rules.name: Colorwheel

evidence.detail: Homestead 1.3.7 ships it in server files; Iris shader support for Flywheel, client rendering only

rules.notes: Iris shaders support for Flywheel. Appears in some packs' server files by author oversight.

rules.name: Colorwheel Patcher

evidence.detail: Homestead 1.3.7: hard-crashes dedicated servers at entrypoint 'main' (ClassNotFoundException on client classes); declares env '*' so Fabric loads it server-side

rules.notes: Patches Flywheel for Iris shader support; its init path touches client-only classes and hard-crashes dedicated servers.

rules.name: Neat

evidence.detail: FTB Evolution 1.21.1 NeoForge server install: Neat-1.21-47-NEOFORGE.jar

rules.notes: Auto-added from FTB Evolution NeoForge server install; verified client-only on Modrinth.

rules.notes: Auto-added from FTB Evolution NeoForge server install; verified client-only on Modrinth.

evidence.detail: FTB Evolution 1.21.1 NeoForge server install: cleanview-1.21.1-v1-neoforge.jar

rules.name: Collapsible Groups (JEI)
rules.reason: JEI UI addon; loads client GUI classes and hard-crashes dedicated servers

evidence.detail: mod 1495382 'Collapsible Groups (JEI)' — a JEI interface addon; CurseForge-only, not published on Modrinth
FTB Evolution NeoForge 21.1.233: 'Attempted to load class net/minecraft/client/gui/screens/Screen for invalid dist DEDICATED_SERVER'

rules.notes: JEI addon for collapsing ingredient groups; client-only, crashes NeoForge dedicated servers at mod construction.
